title: Panel-relative tangent-frame mathematical invariant
question: Does the proposed Gram-Schmidt and fallback construction produce a right-handed orthonormal frame in Blender 5.0.1?
hypothesis: Projection, normalization, cross products, and a least-aligned-axis fallback will remain orthonormal within single-precision tolerance.
assumptions: The tested mathutils vectors represent the vector operations later reproduced by a node builder.
Passing this invariant does not validate imported tangents, carbon-fibre appearance, anisotropic response, or a shader node graph.
procedure: Project the candidate tangent away from the normal.
Use the least-aligned cardinal axis when the tangent is degenerate.
Construct and re-orthogonalize tangent and bitangent with cross products.
Measure unit-length, pairwise-dot, and handedness errors for four cases inside Blender 5.0.1.
acceptance_criteria: Every measured unit-length, orthogonality, and handedness error is no greater than 1e-07.
The parallel tangent case selects a valid deterministic fallback.
failure_criteria: Any case produces a zero, invalid, left-handed, or non-orthogonal frame beyond tolerance.
result: Accepted for the mathematical frame construction only. Four cases passed with maximum recorded error 8.274946261543903e-08.
